title: Gemini API Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers to effectively use the Gemini API for AI chatbot development.

commands.description: Controls randomness in responses, higher values for more creative output.
commands.scenario: When developing a chatbot that needs to generate creative and engaging responses.

commands.description: Limits the number of tokens in the response to control verbosity.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers from your chatbot to save on token usage.

commands.description: Uses the optimized GPT-4 model with streaming for faster response times.
commands.scenario: When building a real-time chatbot application that requires quick responses.

commands.description: Simplifies complex topics for better understanding by non-experts.
commands.scenario: When creating an educational chatbot aimed at young audiences.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues in chatbot response generation.

commands.description: Reduces the likelihood of repeating the same line verbatim.
commands.scenario: When your chatbot tends to repeat itself and you want more varied responses.

commands.description: Encourages the model to talk about new topics.
commands.scenario: When you want your chatbot to explore a wider range of topics in conversation.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages logical reasoning and verification in responses.
commands.scenario: When developing a chatbot that requires accurate and logical problem-solving.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent long waits.
commands.scenario: When integrating the API into applications with strict performance requirements.

commands.description: Returns the log probabilities of top tokens, useful for debugging and analysis.
commands.scenario: When you need to analyze the decision-making process of the model.

commands.description: Retries failed API requests up to three times for reliability.
commands.scenario: When building a robust application that needs to handle transient errors gracefully.

commands.description: Formats responses with markdown for better readability.
commands.scenario: When you want your chatbot to output well-structured and formatted text.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authentication.
commands.scenario: When initializing the API client to authenticate requests.

commands.description: Sets stop sequences to end responses at natural points.
commands.scenario: When you want to ensure responses end cleanly without trailing off.

commands.description: Configure granular safety thresholds to prevent over-refusal in creative or sensitive contexts.

commands.command: system_instruction='You are a specialized Linux kernel expert.'
commands.description: Define a persistent persona and behavioral constraints that remain separate from the user prompt history.
Enable Function Calling to allow the model to output structured JSON for external API execution.
Force the model to output valid JSON, ensuring compatibility with automated parsing pipelines.
Limit the model's vocabulary sampling to the top K most likely tokens to reduce randomness/hallucination.
